scenario: Nimbus Bank runs Camunda 8.8 with 4500 loan instances of orderProcess version 3, some completed, some active, some in incident state, and some terminated. The operations team selects all of them from the process instances list and clicks Migrate to move the cohort to version 4.
question: Which subset of the selected process instances does Operate include in the migration plan?

options.text: All process instances of the source version participate, including completed and terminated ones, so the plan covers the full 4500-instance cohort selected from the list.
Only running process instances, meaning those in active or incident state, participate; all other selected instances are ignored and not part of the migration plan.
Only the process instances currently sitting on a flow node that has been mapped in the bottom panel participate; idle instances are filtered out of the plan.
All selected instances participate, but the completed and terminated ones are flagged for the operator to manually review after the migration finishes.

a.text: Incorrect. Operate does not migrate completed or terminated instances; they are ignored even when the operator selects them in the list. Only running instances reach the migration plan.

b.text: Correct. The migration operation is restricted to running process instances in active or incident state; any other selected instances are excluded from the plan and ignored by the operation.

c.text: Incorrect. Migration eligibility is based on instance lifecycle state, not on the current flow node position; any running instance of the source version qualifies regardless of where its token sits.

d.text: Incorrect. Non-running instances are silently ignored, not flagged for follow-up; the documented behavior is that they simply do not participate in the migration plan.

explanation: In Camunda 8.8 Operate, the migration operation only applies to running process instances, defined as those in an active or incident state. Completed, canceled, or terminated instances selected from the list are silently excluded from the migration plan.
